body.title: CLOSED: batch cortex writes in _deposit_nodes
body.result: batch cortex API (store_batch/add_children_batch/add_interpretive_edges_batch) — _deposit_nodes O(N)→O(1) DB round-trips
body.description:
_deposit_nodes in book_learner.py issues up to 7 DB round-trips per node (store, embed, add_child x2, add_interpretive_edge, attractor query, habit query). For a 200-chunk book at 3 nodes/chunk that is 4000+ round-trips per book. Wrap each chunk's deposits in a single transaction; batch the cortex.store calls using execute_values or equivalent; batch add_child/add_interpretive_edge similarly. Cortex may need new store_batch() / add_children_batch() methods. Rollback story: batching improves atomicity — a failed chunk does not commit at all (strictly better than current per-node partial writes).

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/book_learner.py, wild_igor/igor/memory/cortex.py (new batch methods, existing untouched)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite, test-plan-or-why-not
**Scope boundary:** IN: batch the DB writes per chunk; add cortex batch API. OUT: do not change per-node extraction logic, node schema, or arousal assignment.
**Test plan:** existing book_learner tests must still pass; add a test that a 10-node batch write is one transaction (use a mock or count query calls).
**Pass condition:** A 200-chunk book processes in materially fewer DB round-trips; pytest passes; no partial-chunk writes on simulated failure.
